name: Fonz y Bajo Cinca – circular from Monzón
name_original: Fonz y Bajo Cinca – circular desde Monzón
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Monzón, located in the province of Huesca, Spain, traverse a landscape characterized by varied terrain, historical landmarks, and significant waterways. The area features mostly paved surfaces, accommodating road cyclists across different skill levels. Routes often incorporate historical sites such as Monzón Castle and the Albarda Medieval Bridge, alongside natural features like the Cinca River and the Canal of Aragon and Catalonia. The region offers a mix of elevation gains, from relatively flat sections to more challenging climbs. Best road cycling routes around Monzón The most popular road cycling route is Fonz y Bajo Cinca – circular from Monzón, a 49.3 miles (79.3 km) trail that takes 3 hours 18 minutes to complete. This route offers a moderate challenge through the surrounding agricultural areas. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Co-cathedral – Albarda Medieval Bridge loop from Monzón-Río Cinca, a difficult 78.1 miles (125.7 km) path. This route features significant historical sites like the Co-cathedral and the Albarda Medieval Bridge, alongside stretches along the Cinca River. Local road cyclists also love the Puente de Abajo – Capella Romanesque Bridge loop from Monzón-Río Cinca, a 77.8 miles (125.3 km) trail leading through riverine landscapes and past the Capella Romanesque Bridge, often completed in about 5 hours 58 minutes. Road cycling around Monzón is defined by historical sites, river valleys, and varied elevation profiles, offering options for different ability levels. The routes in Monzón are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 800 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Monzón's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Monzón?
A: Monzón offers a diverse selection of 17 road cycling routes, catering to various skill levels. These include 3 easy routes, 10 moderate routes, and 4 more challenging options.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Monzón?
A: The terrain around Monzón is varied, featuring mostly paved surfaces suitable for road cycling. You'll find routes with different elevation gains, from relatively flat sections with up to 500 ft of elevation to more demanding climbs exceeding 1500 ft, allowing for both leisurely rides and more intense training.

Q: Are there any family-friendly road cycling routes in Monzón?
A: Yes, Monzón has 3 easy road cycling routes that are generally suitable for families or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation and shorter distances, providing a pleasant experience for all ages.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see along the road cycling routes in Monzón?
A: Many routes in Monzón incorporate significant historical sites. You can cycle past the impressive &lt;strong&gt;Monzón Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, a Templar fortress (&lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5031011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón Castle&lt;/a&gt;), or encounter the &lt;strong&gt;Co-cathedral of Santa María del Romeral&lt;/strong&gt;. The ancient &lt;strong&gt;Albarda Medieval Bridge&lt;/strong&gt;, one of the oldest in the Guara-Somontano region, is also featured on some routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24318350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Co-cathedral – Albarda Medieval Bridge loop from Monzón-Río Cinca&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any natural attractions or scenic spots along the cycling paths?
A: Absolutely. Cyclists can enjoy scenic stretches alongside the &lt;strong&gt;Canal of Aragon and Catalonia&lt;/strong&gt;, an important historical engineering feat. The &lt;strong&gt;Cinca River&lt;/strong&gt; also features prominently in routes around Monzón, contributing to the natural beauty of the area. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10466778" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de Abajo – Capella Romanesque Bridge loop from Monzón-Río Cinca&lt;/a&gt;, for example, leads through beautiful riverine landscapes.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Monzón?
A: While specific seasonal data isn't provided, the varied terrain and historical sites suggest that spring and autumn would likely offer the most comfortable temperatures for road cycling. Summers can be hot, and winters might be cooler, so checking local weather forecasts is always recommended for optimal conditions.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Monzón?
A: Yes, many of Monzón's road cycling routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e930666301/fonz-y-bajo-cinca-circular-from-monzon" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fonz y Bajo Cinca – circular from Monzón&lt;/a&gt;, which offers a moderate challenge through agricultural areas.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Monzón?
A: The road cycling routes in Monzón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse terrain, the blend of historical sites with natural scenery, and the well-maintained paved surfaces that make for an enjoyable ride.

Q: Where can I find parking facilities for road cycling routes in Monzón?
A: While specific parking locations for each route are not detailed, Monzón, as a town, generally offers various parking options. For routes starting directly from Monzón, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38278158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón Castle – Parish Church of Fonz loop from Monzón-Río Cinca&lt;/a&gt;, you would typically find parking within or on the outskirts of the town.

Q: Are there any particularly challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Monzón offers 4 difficult road cycling routes. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24318350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Co-cathedral – Albarda Medieval Bridge loop from Monzón-Río Cinca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 78.1 miles (125.7 km) and features significant elevation changes, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience.

Q: Can I access road cycling routes using public transport in Monzón?
A: Monzón has public transport options, but specific details on transporting bicycles or direct access points to routes via public transport are not provided. It's advisable to check local bus or train services for their bicycle policies if you plan to use public transport to reach your starting point.

Q: Are there any other towns or settlements I can visit while road cycling near Monzón?
A: Yes, some routes may lead you through or near other settlements. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40639141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monzón Castle – Parish Church of Fonz loop from Selgua&lt;/a&gt; can take you towards Fonz. Additionally, nearby towns like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430423" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barbastro&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5430528" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berbegal&lt;/a&gt; are within the broader region and offer their own attractions.
tips: The emblematic Plaza Mayor has been declared a Historic-Artistic Monument, it houses the Renaissance building of the Town Hall and the magnificent 'fountain of the six spouts', built in stone in the 16th century. Another distinctive feature of Fonz is the abundance of constructions related to water.

Its spectacular Templar castle has witnessed ten centuries of a hectic military history.

It was erected by the Muslims; later it would be the seat of the main Templar encomienda of the Crown of Aragon, where the infant Jaime I, future king of Aragon, was educated. Currently, the castle houses an interesting Interpretation Center where you will be introduced to the fascinating world of the warrior-monks of the Order of the Temple.
